matches.rationale: Same Bash target: before creating a pull request after library changes, the extracted rule requires the library-local make checks and explicitly includes make format. It also bundles make lint and make test, but under the 1:1 rule it is assigned to this single gold rule.
Same Edit target: the pattern blocks Sphinx-style double-backtick inline code, and the evidence expressly limits the instruction to inline code references in docstrings and comments.

misses.note: The only Bash extraction bundles make lint with format and test in one rule and is already consumed by g1; 1:1 matching forbids reusing it.
The only Bash extraction bundles make test with format and lint in one rule and is already consumed by g1; 1:1 matching forbids reusing it.
Compiler self-reported skip: "TEST env var syntax for scoping `make test` to a specific file/pytest options — usage documentation, not an obligation or prohibition."
The extracted Edit rule's evidence includes the single-backtick requirement, but that rule is already consumed by the closer g5 prohibition and cannot be reused under 1:1 matching.
